hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` card declares one explicit `first_broken_law` drawn from the authoritative `/work/xr` gap surface list, so route-neutral system work cannot optimize against vague XR wording.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `sysopt.xr-*` cards could bind to the authoritative `/work/xr` gap artifact yet still stay vague about which named broken law they were tightening, so route-neutral system work could drift away from the first measured XR failure. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` rejects any `sysopt.xr-*` card that omits `first_broken_law` or names a law outside `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json.top_drift_surfaces`, and the fixed promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails XR cards that omit `first_broken_law` or target a non-authoritative XR drift surface, and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR systems-optimizer ratchets must bind to one named broken law from the authoritative gap surface list, or evaluator tightening still leaves route-neutral work too vague to falsify.
